# Copilot Instructions — Nuxt 4 Project

## Project Identity

This is a **Nuxt 4** full-stack application built with:

- **Framework:** Nuxt 4.x (Vue 3, Nitro, Vite)
- **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode, full-stack)
- **Server Engine:** Nitro (built into Nuxt 4) — serves API routes + static SPA
- **Database:** PostgreSQL 16
- **ORM:** Drizzle ORM (with drizzle-kit for migrations)
- **Validation:** Zod (shared between server & client)
- **Authentication:** JWT via `jose` library (HS256)
- **Real-Time:** Centrifugo WebSocket server
- **Queue / Jobs:** BullMQ (Redis-backed)
- **Cache:** Redis (shared with BullMQ)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 4 (dark theme, custom violet/purple palette)
- **State:** Pinia (Setup Stores)
- **Animations:** GSAP 3.13 (ScrollTrigger, ScrollToPlugin)
- **Drag & Drop:** vue-draggable-plus v4
- **Icons:** vue-iconsax v2 + @nuxt/icon + Material Symbols
- **Toasts:** vue-sonner
- **Utilities:** VueUse composables
- **Testing:** Vitest (unit/component) + Playwright (e2e)
- **Linting:** ESLint + Prettier
- **Deployment:** Docker container (self-hosted)
- **Architecture:** Full-stack — Nitro server handles API routes, Drizzle ORM for PostgreSQL, Redis for caching/queues

## Spec-Driven Development (SDD) — Hard Gate

**No code may be generated without a corresponding spec file in `specs/`.**

Before writing any implementation code, you MUST:

1. Check if a spec exists in `specs/` for the feature/task
2. If no spec exists, create one using the `write-spec` prompt OR ask the developer to provide one
3. Reference the spec file path in a comment at the top of every new file: `// Spec: specs/<feature-name>.md`

The only exception is `fix-bug` tasks, which may proceed without a spec.

## Nuxt 4 Directory Structure

Nuxt 4 uses the `app/` directory for client code and `server/` for the API backend:

```
project-root/
├── app/                              # SPA client (Vue 3)
│   ├── assets/                       # Uncompiled assets (CSS, images, fonts)
│   ├── components/                   # Auto-imported Vue components
│   ├── composables/                  # Auto-imported composables
│   │   └── api/                      # Internal API composables
│   ├── layouts/                      # Layout components
│   ├── middleware/                    # Route middleware
│   ├── pages/                        # File-based routing
│   ├── plugins/                      # Nuxt plugins
│   ├── stores/                       # Pinia stores
│   ├── types/                        # Client-side types
│   ├── utils/                        # Auto-imported utility functions
│   ├── app.vue                       # Root component
│   ├── app.config.ts                 # App-level runtime config
│   └── error.vue                     # Error page
├── server/                           # Nitro server (API backend)
│   ├── api/                          # API route handlers (file-based routing)
│   │   ├── auth/                     # Authentication routes
│   │   ├── admin/                    # Admin-only routes
│   │   └── client/                   # Client-facing routes
│   ├── database/                     # Database layer
│   │   ├── schema/                   # Drizzle ORM table schemas + relations
│   │   ├── migrations/               # Generated by drizzle-kit
│   │   ├── seed.ts                   # Database seeder
│   │   └── index.ts                  # Drizzle client instance (db)
│   ├── middleware/                    # Server middleware (auth, admin, active-user)
│   ├── services/                     # Business logic services
│   ├── transformers/                 # Response transformers (replaces Laravel Resources)
│   ├── jobs/                         # BullMQ job definitions
│   ├── utils/                        # Server utilities (jwt, password, response, broadcast)
│   └── plugins/                      # Nitro plugins (BullMQ worker init)
├── shared/                           # Shared between server & client
│   ├── enums/                        # TypeScript const enums
│   ├── schemas/                      # Zod validation schemas
│   ├── types/                        # Shared TypeScript interfaces
│   └── utils/                        # Shared utility functions
├── public/                           # Static assets (served as-is)
├── specs/                            # Feature specifications (SDD)
├── tests/
│   ├── unit/                         # Vitest unit tests (mirrors app/ + server/ structure)
│   └── e2e/                          # Playwright e2e tests
├── nuxt.config.ts                    # Nuxt configuration
├── drizzle.config.ts                 # Drizzle ORM configuration
├── tailwind.config.js                # Tailwind configuration
├── tsconfig.json                     # TypeScript configuration
├── compose.dev.yaml                  # Docker dev orchestration
└── compose.prod.yaml                 # Docker prod orchestration
```

## File Naming Conventions

### Client-side
- **Components:** `kebab-case.vue` → `board-card.vue`
- **Composables:** `use-<name>.ts` → `use-board-api.ts`
- **Stores:** `<domain>.ts` → `auth.ts`, `board.ts`
- **Pages:** `kebab-case.vue` following Nuxt file-based routing
- **Types:** `<domain>.types.ts` → `board.types.ts`
- **Tests:** `<source-file>.test.ts` (unit), `<feature>.spec.ts` (e2e)
- **Specs:** `<feature-name>.md` in `specs/`

### Server-side
- **Route handlers:** `<method>.ts` → `all.get.ts`, `create.post.ts`, `[id].put.ts`, `[id].delete.ts`
- **Database schemas:** `<entity>.ts` → `boards.ts`, `cards.ts`
- **Transformers:** `<entity>.transformer.ts` → `board.transformer.ts`
- **Services:** `<domain>.ts` → `notification.ts`, `centrifugo.ts`
- **Jobs:** `<name>.job.ts` → `notification.job.ts`

### Shared
- **Enums:** `<Entity>Enum.ts` → `BoardStatusEnum.ts`, `UserRoleEnum.ts`
- **Zod schemas:** `<entity>.schema.ts` → `board.schema.ts`, `card.schema.ts`

## TypeScript Rules

- Strict mode is mandatory (`strict: true` in tsconfig)
- No `any` — use `unknown` with type guards when truly necessary
- No `@ts-ignore` or `@ts-expect-error` without a linked issue comment
- Use `interface` for object shapes, `type` for unions and intersections
- All composables must have explicit return types
- Use Zod for runtime validation of API request bodies and responses
- Use `const` objects with `as const` for enums (not TypeScript `enum`)

## Vue / Nuxt Conventions

- Always use `<script setup lang="ts">` — no Options API
- Props via `defineProps<T>()` with an interface
- Emits via `defineEmits<T>()`
- Prefer Nuxt auto-imports (Vue, VueUse, Pinia, Nuxt utilities)
- Use explicit imports only for third-party libraries
- No business logic in `<template>` — extract to composables
- Use `definePageMeta()` for page-level metadata
- Use `NuxtLink` instead of `<a>` or `router-link`

## Tailwind CSS Rules

- Utility-first: no custom CSS unless absolutely necessary
- Use `@apply` sparingly and only in component `<style>` blocks
- Design tokens defined in `tailwind.config.js`
- Responsive: mobile-first (`sm:`, `md:`, `lg:`)
- Dark mode via `class` strategy (dark theme is the default)

## Data Fetching Rules (Client-Side)

- Use `useFetch()` or `useAsyncData()` for all data fetching from internal API routes
- No raw `fetch()` calls in components
- All API calls go through composables in `app/composables/api/`
- Use relative paths for internal Nitro routes: `useFetch('/api/client/board/all')`
- Validate API responses with Zod schemas
- `useFetch` for reads, `$fetch` for mutations (POST/PUT/DELETE)
- Note: `useFetch`/`useAsyncData` return `undefined` (not `null`) as defaults in Nuxt 4

## Server-Side Rules

- All API route handlers use `defineEventHandler()` in `server/api/`
- File-based routing: `server/api/client/board/all.get.ts` → `GET /api/client/board/all`
- Request body validation via Zod schemas from `shared/schemas/`
- Database access via Drizzle ORM — never raw SQL
- All query results pass through transformer functions before returning
- Server middleware in `server/middleware/`: `auth.ts`, `admin.ts`, `active-user.ts`
- JWT operations via `jose` library in `server/utils/jwt.ts`
- Password hashing via bcrypt in `server/utils/password.ts`
- Broadcasts to Centrifugo via `server/utils/broadcast.ts`
- Background jobs via BullMQ in `server/jobs/`

## State Management Rules

- Pinia Setup Stores syntax (not Options)
- One store per domain — stores live in `app/stores/`
- Components should prefer composables over direct store access
- No store-to-store direct dependencies — use composables as mediators

## Quality Gates

- ESLint + Prettier must pass with zero errors
- All new features require at minimum: happy path test + one error case test
- E2e tests for user-facing flows
- No console.log in committed code (use proper logging)

## Agent System

This project uses a multi-agent system. See `AGENTS.md` for agent roles and workflow.
Contextual instructions are in `.github/instructions/`.
Reusable prompts are in `.github/prompts/`.
Agent definitions are in `.github/agents/`.
Skills reference is in `.github/skills/`.
